text.content:
The Scene App is the Narrator's main tracker: shared scene tags & statuses, dice-roll modifiers, the characters present with their selected tags, plus journeys and story themes. Tags you add here flow live into the players' roll dialogs. Tags are always listed before statuses.

Opening it

Use the Mist Engine toolbar group on the left (smog icon) → Scene Tags, or press Ctrl+J. Requires an active scene.

Legend in the Mist for Foundry VTT. Almost every input in this system shows a short help text when you hover it.

Guided Tours

Two guided tours ship with the system: the Narrator Tour (GM tools) and the Hero Tour (character sheet & rolling). Each starts automatically on first login and can be replayed any time via Settings → Tour Management.

Feedback

Found a bug? Open an issue on the GitHub page or reach us on the Sons of Oak Discord (#vtt), also via DM.

Recommended modules

  • Dice So Nice!

  • Tokenizer: The Token Editor

The fellowship shares one themecard, technically a separate actor owned by all fellowship players.

  1. Create an actor of type Fellowship Themecard.

  2. Give all fellowship players ownership of that actor.

  3. Assign each character to their player (User Configuration). Do not assign the themecard actor itself to anyone.

Then create or assign the card from a hero's character sheet — every connected hero sees the same card and can use its tags in rolls. Reflecting during camping can improve it like any theme.

The roll dialog is reactive: open it with Quick, Detailed or Reaction Roll (the Sacrifice button has its own small dialog), then (de)select tags & statuses on the character sheet or the scene — the dialog updates and totals your Power live.

  • Burn a tag: click the burn icon next to a power tag (+3 Power for that one roll, then scratched until recovered).

  • Weaknesses count −1 Power. With the Narrator's blessing, click a selected weakness in the dialog to invert it: +1 Power for this roll only (marked in the chat card and the GM confirmation).

  • Challenge statuses can be flipped against the roll for that single roll — without editing the challenge.

  • Request Help: if another player is online, this button asks fellow heroes to contribute one tag each (+1 Power, cannot be burned).

  • Might scale (optional setting) adds −6…+6.

Detailed action — spending Power

On a successful Detailed action the chat card offers an interactive Spend Power menu (Core Book p. 154): add/scratch a tag (2), status per tier (1), discover (1), feat (1), single-use (1) — with undo, synced to all clients. Only the roller or the GM may spend.

GM Roll Confirmation

Optional setting: player rolls are first sent to the Narrator, who sees the full selection and approves or rejects before the dice fall.

The compendium LiTM Themebooks contains every themebook from the Core Book (without the optional power-tag questions, see p. 92). Drag a themebook onto a character sheet in Edit Mode to add it. It also contains a ready-to-use Backpack — drag it onto a character.

ThemeKits

ThemeKits are prebuilt tag packages. Open the ThemeKit selection via the character sheet header button or Alt+T, pick a kit and it is imported into the character. Two settings control where kits are pulled from: ThemeKit source compendiums and include world items.

If you want to create a macro for showing the scene tags dialog. Use this code

MistSceneApp.getInstance().render(true, { focus: true })

If you want to create a macro for showing the how to play dialog. Use this code

HowToPlayApp.getInstance().render(true, { focus: true })

Moving themebooks in the character sheet

You can right click on themebook's title in a character sheet to toggle the context menu for moving the themebook from the Main tab to the Other tab and reverse

Tags & Statuses

You can right click a tag in the Tags & Statuses Box of characters, challenges and the scene app to set the tag as a mighty tag.

You can import complete challenges, short challenges and vignettes via a custom json import. Enable via the config settings the JSON import and you will see the corresponding buttons.

The JSON format is defined here: https://github.com/MrTheBino/mist-engine-fvtt/wiki/Custom-JSON-Import

Use these tokens in almost every text field (sheets, journals, chat) to render Mist-engine style marks. The marks are draggable into sheets and rolls.

  • [tag] — a simple tag

  • [status-1] — a status with tier 1–6

  • [/s status] / [/s status-2] — status without / with tier

  • [/w weakness] — a weakness tag

  • [/l limit] / [/l limit-4] — limit without / with value

  • [/m might] — a might word; [/ma …], [/mg …], [/mo …] for adventure / greatness / origin

  • [/b text] — bold

The rich-text editor toolbar has matching buttons, and Alt+B converts the selected text in the editor. In plain input fields use the Ctrl+Shift shortcuts (see Hotkeys).

Global

Ctrl+JOpen Scene Tags AppEveryone
Ctrl+HOpen How To PlayEveryone
Alt+TOpen ThemeKit selection for your characterPlayers

Text fields

Select text in an input or textarea first, then press:

Ctrl+Shift+AWrap as Tag […]
Ctrl+Shift+SWrap as Status [/s …]
Ctrl+Shift+XWrap as Might [/m …]
Ctrl+Shift+YWrap as Bold [/b …]

In the rich-text editor, Alt+B converts the selected text into tag markup.

Canvas

Ctrl+HoverShow a token's tags & statuses overlayGM only

Helping Each Other

While one hero rolls, they can press Request Help. Every other player gets a dialog to contribute one of their tags: +1 Power each, contributed tags cannot be burned. Contributions are listed in the roll and the chat card.

Acting Together

The Narrator starts a group action via the Mist Engine toolbar. Each hero contributes one tag (+1 Power); a single hero may burn a tag (+3). The Narrator can add a modifier; one roll resolves the action for the whole group.

Camping mode (Core Book p. 179) runs from the Mist Engine toolbar (campground icon, Narrator only, needs an active scene). Choose Camp or Sojourn plus a duration — sojourns grant +1/+2/+3 bonus Power on camp actions (days/weeks/months).

  • A camp has two periods; the Narrator may open a risky third.

  • Each hero once per camp: Rest (expire one of their statuses), Reflect (improve a theme — also the fellowship themecard), and a Camp Action (a roll with the sojourn bonus, or spend half their Power without rolling).

  • Fellowship quality time: recover a scratched relationship tag or create a new one.

  • Campsite tags are floating tags/statuses on the scene, usable in camp rolls.

  • Backpack review: mark story tags as expiring, then apply the expiration; the Narrator can recreate expired items later.

Every activity is posted to chat so the whole table follows along.

System settings (Configure Settings → Mist Engine):

  • GM Roll Confirmation — player rolls need Narrator approval first.

  • Might Usage — enables the Might scale (−6…+6) in the roll dialog.

  • Disable LiTM Custom Dice — use plain d6 instead of the custom faces.

  • Disable Character Hover Tooltip — turns off the Ctrl+Hover token overlay.

  • Show Custom JSON Import — shows the JSON import buttons (see JSON Import).

  • Tidy Tags on Character Sheet — compacter tag display.

  • ThemeKit source compendiums / include world items — where the ThemeKit selection pulls its kits from.
